title: Fragment: 'Wake The Serpent Not'.
content:
Wake the serpent not - lest he
    Should not know the way to go, -
    Let him crawl which yet lies sleeping
    Through the deep grass of the meadow!
    Not a bee shall hear him creeping,
    Not a may-fly shall awaken
    From its cradling blue-bell shaken,
    Not the starlight as he's sliding
    Through the grass with silent gliding.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Percy Bysshe Shelley, titled "Fragment: 'Wake The Serpent Not'.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
